source.catalog_ref: HV21 (user-assigned international combat-robot league; catalog HV20)

full_prompt: Design a monolithic AR500 wear-resistant alloy-steel horizontal-spinner weapon disc for a international combat-robot league 2026 Middleweight class (30 lb / 13.6 kg) combat robot, per international combat-robot league Combat Robot rules for weight class limits, power-on-light requirements, and BattleBots-adjacent good-engineering-practice. The spinner disc is the primary weapon, mounted on a vertical central drive shaft and spun up by a brushless outrunner to ~9000 rpm in a 3-minute match, delivering impact kinetic energy to opposing robots through 2 integral tooth features. The weapon disc is a single monolithic waterjet-cut and surface-ground AR500 plate (no welds, no bolted-on teeth; integral 2 teeth + integral central shaft bore + integral balancing-fluting). Geometry: 220 mm outer diameter x 14 mm thick steel disc with 2 integral teeth spaced at 180 deg; each tooth is a 25 mm long x 30 mm wide x 14 mm thick protrusion extending outward from the 210 mm base circle; the tooth leading edge is a 2 mm radius fillet (hardened impact zone); each tooth trailing face has a 45 deg rake. Central shaft bore: 20 mm diameter keyed hub (matching a 20 mm hardened shaft with 6 mm keyway), reinforced with 4 integral M6 clamping-bolt holes on a 40 mm bolt-circle to share torque with the shaft key. Eight integral balancing flutes (6 mm diameter x 4 mm deep blind pockets) on the non-tooth side for dynamic balance trimming. Overall bounding envelope 220 x 220 x 14 mm. Material AR500: E 210 GPa, nu 0.30, density 7850 kg/m^3, yield 1380 MPa, UTS 1620 MPa, hardness 500 HB through-thickness. Three load cases per combat-robot weapon design practice. Load Case 1 (centrifugal body loading at 9000 rpm): 9000 rpm = 942 rad/s, peak tip speed 104 m/s, produces radial centrifugal body force density rho*omega^2*r integrated through the disc + teeth; this is the pure spin case with no impact. Load Case 2 (single-tooth impact on 3 mm aluminum opponent armor): 8 kN tangential force on one tooth tip for 300 microseconds peak contact (2.4 J per strike), applied 100 times in a 3-minute match at 9000 rpm matches time between impacts; load reacted at the shaft bore through the key and clamp bolts; the disc is still spinning at 9000 rpm (centrifugal + impact superposition). Load Case 3 (catastrophic hammer-to-hardened-steel hit): 40 kN tangential impulse on one tooth representing striking a hardened-steel opposing weapon at full speed (rare, fault-case, tooth may deform but must not fracture catastrophically); disc still spinning at 9000 rpm. Pass/fail: (R1) under LC1 pure spin, peak von Mises must not exceed 690 MPa (yield/2.0 AR500); (R2) under LC2 tooth impact during spin, peak stress must not exceed 920 MPa (yield/1.5 transient) and no fracture (use max-principal stress < UTS/1.5 = 1080 MPa as a secondary gate); (R3) under LC3 hardened-steel hit, peak stress <= UTS 1620 MPa (allowed plastic deformation, no through-crack); (R4) first six natural frequencies (spinning disc with lumped shaft-bearing stiffness boundary) must exceed 700 Hz to stay above the 150 Hz motor controller PWM ripple and the 42 Hz + harmonics of the chassis-drive vibration; (R5) critical spin-burst speed: linear buckling eigenvalue analysis of the disc under centrifugal loading must show the first buckling mode occur above 13500 rpm (50% above operating 9000 rpm), preventing disc burst within the match envelope; (R6) spinner mass must not fall below 2.2 kg nor exceed 4.0 kg (too light = low KE, too heavy = can't reach 9000 rpm in 45 s spin-up; competitive combat weapons target 2.5-3.5 kg); (R7) static imbalance moment with balancing flutes must be correctable to below 5 g-mm (weapon disc imbalance above this damages bearings within one match). Thermal analysis: weapon-disc impact heating is localized and surface only; no thermal gate required. Fluid analysis not required (air drag handled as torque budget, not structural load). Deliver STEP AP242 geometry plus report.
short_prompt:
Design a monolithic AR500 wear-resistant alloy-steel horizontal-spinner weapon disc for an international combat-robot league Middleweight (30 lb / 13.6 kg) entry, spun up to ~9000 rpm in a 3-minute match. The disc is a single waterjet-cut and surface-ground AR500 plate with two integral 180-degree-spaced teeth (2 mm leading-edge fillet, 45-degree rake on the trailing face), a central keyed 20 mm shaft bore with four M6 clamp-bolts on 40 mm BCD, and eight blind balancing flutes on the non-tooth face. Apply yield/2.0 continuous and yield/1.5 transient safety factors with UTS/1.5 principal-stress gate; catastrophic LC3 is a fault case (plastic deformation allowed, no through-crack).

Envelope: 220 x 220 x 14 mm.

Load cases.
- LC1 pure spin at 9000 rpm (942 rad/s, 104 m/s tip speed): centrifugal body loading rho*omega^2*r integrated through disc and teeth.
- LC2 single-tooth impact on 3 mm aluminum armor during spin: 8 kN tangential for 300 us (2.4 J/strike), 100 strikes/match, superposed on 9000 rpm spin.
- LC3 catastrophic hardened-steel hit during spin: 40 kN tangential impulse on one tooth, still 9000 rpm.
Also require a spin-burst buckling factor above 13500 rpm (50% margin), first six modes >700 Hz, mass 2.2-4.0 kg, and residual static imbalance below 5 g-mm.

prompt.title: international combat-robot league middleweight horizontal-spinner weapon disc
prompt.brief: AR500 220x14 mm 2-tooth horizontal spinner at 9000 rpm; centrifugal + tooth-impact + burst-speed gates.

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: second-order tetrahedral (TET10) + contact for tooth impact
analysis_types: linear static with centrifugal body load (LC1)

exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Air drag is torque budget, not structural load.
